perturbation.original_text: Unless otherwise defined, capitalised terms, singular or plural, used in this Amendment, shall have the same meaning ascribed thereto in the GMA.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless otherwise defined in this Amendment, capitalized terms, singular or plural, used in this Amendment, shall have the same meaning ascribed thereto in the GMA, unless explicitly redefined within a specific clause of this Amendment. However, the definition of a term in the GMA will not be applicable if the term is redefined in this Amendment.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that capitalized terms have the same meaning as in the GMA unless redefined in a specific clause within the Amendment. This directly contradicts the original statement that terms have the same meaning as in the GMA regardless of whether the definition is present or not. This creates uncertainty as to which definition applies.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In case of any inconsistency between the terms of the GMA and this Amendment regarding the subject-matter, the latter shall prevail. To the extent not inconsistent with this Amendment, all terms and conditions of the GMA shall remain valid and binding.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction because it states that capitalized terms have the same meaning as in the GMA unless redefined in a specific clause within the Amendment. This directly contradicts the original statement that terms have the same meaning as in the GMA regardless of whether the definition is present or not. The contradicted text states that 'In case of any inconsistency between the terms of the GMA and this Amendment regarding the subject-matter, the latter shall prevail' but the new text creates a new inconsistency regarding the application of definitions of terms found in the GMA or the Amendment.

perturbation.original_text: This Amendment shall not be varied or modified except by a written document duly signed by duly authorized representatives of both Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Amendment shall not be varied or modified except by a written document duly signed by duly authorized representatives of both Parties. However, operational changes can be implemented based on verbal agreements between the project managers of both parties, these verbal agreements do not need to be reflected within a written document.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that operational changes can be implemented based on verbal agreements, despite the previous sentence clearly stating that all modifications must be in writing and signed by authorized representatives. This contradiction creates ambiguity and uncertainty as to which method of modification is valid. Making the contract vague and susceptible to different interpretaions and challenges in court.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Amendment shall not be varied or modified except by a written document duly signed by duly authorized representatives of both Parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly allows for verbal agreements to modify the contract for operational changes, directly contradicting the original clause (and the contradicted text, which is the same) that states all modifications must be in writing and signed by authorized representatives. This introduces a clear in-text contradiction, as per the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition, making the contract ambiguous and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: Any Confidential Information shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party. The Amendment shall not be construed as granting or conferring to the Receiving Party, either expressly or by implication, any license or proprietary interest in or to any Confidential Information nor any right of use beyond the purpose of this Amendment. The Repairer, its Affiliates and/or its Subcontractors as applicable shall remain the exclusive owner of any intellectual property right related to the Services including: design of the LRUs, Main Elements, job cards, task cards, operating manual or industrial process, as relevant.
perturbation.changed_text: Any Confidential Information shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party, except for information shared during regular project status meetings, which can be freely used by either party. The Amendment shall not be construed as granting or conferring to the Receiving Party, either expressly or by implication, any license or proprietary interest in or to any Confidential Information nor any right of use beyond the purpose of this Amendment. The Repairer, its Affiliates and/or its Subcontractors as applicable shall remain the exclusive owner of any intellectual property right related to the Services including: design of the LRUs, Main Elements, job cards, task cards, operating manual or industrial process, as relevant.
perturbation.explanation: This modification introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that while confidential information remains the property of the disclosing party, information shared during regular project status meetings can be freely used by either party. This directly contradicts the concept that *all* confidential information remains the property of the disclosing party and creates ambiguity around which information is actually protected under the confidentiality clause. It would be impossible to determine what information is considered confidential versus non-confidential since everything is being discussed during the project status meeting.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidentiality obligations Unless otherwise provided in this Amendment, any Confidential Information released by either of the Parties (the "Disclosing Party") to the other Party (the "Receiving Party") shall not be released in whole or in part to any third party. In particular, the Receiving Party undertakes: - to keep the Confidential Information strictly confidential, not to deliver, disclose or publish it to any third party including subsidiarycompanies and companies having an interest in its capital, except as otherwise agreed in writing by the Disclosing Party; - to use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose of this Amendment and except as otherwise expressly agreed in writing bythe Disclosing Party, not to use the same or permit its use for any other purpose; - to disclose the Confidential Information only to those of its direct employees having a need to know such Confidential Information in order to make permitted use thereof, after having beforehand clearly informed such employees of the strictly confidential nature of the Confidential Information and caused them to observe said conditions of confidentiality.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states that 'Any Confidential Information shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party.' The changed text modifies this by adding 'except for information shared during regular project status meetings, which can be freely used by either party.' This directly contradicts the original statement and creates a conflict with the contradicted text, which mandates that 'any Confidential Information released by either of the Parties...shall not be released in whole or in part to any third party' and 'to keep the Confidential Information strictly confidential.' The changed text permits the free use of information shared in project status meetings, regardless of its confidential nature, directly violating the confidentiality obligations outlined in the contradicted text.
